lines: Tunisia have now shipped four goals to Japan and been blanked twice — Callum and Dene, your Tunisian adventure is officially over. It lasted about as long as a Greggs sausage roll at halftime.
The Foreman's Curaçao managed a 0-0 with Ecuador yesterday, which is technically a point, but let's be honest — celebrating that is like being proud you didn't fall over getting out of bed.
Al's Netherlands absolutely battered Jim's Sweden 5-1, which means Al is quietly sitting pretty while Jim watches his Group F hopes drain away alongside his self-respect.

Today's big one: Spain vs Saudi Arabia — Jim needs his Spaniards to wake up after that opening draw, while Wilmot is praying Saudi Arabia pull off a miracle that absolutely nobody outside of Wilmot's living room believes is coming.

Keep an eye on Ellis's New Zealand vs Egypt in Group G — every team in that group has one point each, so it's genuinely wide open, and Ellis desperately needs the Kiwis to do something useful for once in their footballing lives.
